provisions.chapter: SECTION E. Incompatibilities
provisions.title: Article 132
provisions.content: Members of the Executive Power and the Director Generals of Government departments may not be elected members of the Legislature unless they resign at least one (1) year before the date of the elections.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER III. The Executive Power
provisions.title: Article 133
provisions.content:
The Executive power is vested in:
the President of the Republic, who is the Head of State.
the Government, which is headed by a Prime Minister.

provisions.chapter: SECTION A. The President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 134
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
Claim of universal suffrage, Head of state selection
The President of the Republic is elected by direct, universal suffrage by the absolute majority of voters, established from the valid votes in accordance with the electoral law. If this majority is not obtained at the first round, it proceeds to a second round. Only the two (2) candidates can be presented who, the case arising, after the withdrawal of more favored candidates, who are determined to have received the greatest number of votes in the first round.

provisions.chapter: SECTION A. The President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 134-1
provisions.content: The term of the President is five (5) years. This term begins and ends on the February 7 following the date of the elections.

provisions.chapter: SECTION A. The President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 134-2
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
Scheduling of elections
The presidential election takes place on the last Sunday of October of the fifth year of the presidential mandate.
The president elected enters into his functions on 7 February following the date of his election. In the case where the ballot cannot take place before 7 February, the president elected enters into his functions immediately after the validation of the ballot and his mandate is considered to have commenced on 7 February of the year of the election.
Head of state term limits

provisions.chapter: SECTION A. The President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 134-3
provisions.content: The President of the Republic may not be re-elected. He may serve an additional term only after an interval of five (5) years. He may in no case run for a third term.

provisions.chapter: SECTION A. The President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 135

provisions.chapter: SECTION A. The President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 135-1
provisions.content: Before taking office, the President of the Republic shall take the following oath before the National Assembly: “I swear before God and the Nation faithfully to observe and enforce the Constitution and the laws of the Republic, to respect and cause to be respected the rights of the Haitian people, to work for the greatness of the country, and to maintain the nation’s independence and the integrity of its territory”.

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 136
provisions.content: The President of the Republic, who is the Head of State, shall see to the respect for and enforcement of the Constitution and the stability of the institutions. He shall ensure the regular operations of the public authorities and the continuity of the State.

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 137
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
Head of government's role in the legislature, Head of government selection
The President of the Republic chooses a Prime Minister from among the members of the Party having the absolute majority in the Parliament. The majority is established on the basis of the electoral results of those elected in each of the two Chambers. In default of this majority, the President of the Republic chooses the Prime Minister in consultation with the President of the Senate and that of the Chamber of Deputies.

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 137-1
provisions.content: The President of the Republic shall terminate the duties of the Prime Minister upon the letter’s submission of the Government’s resignation.

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 138
provisions.content:
The President of the Republic is the guarantor of the nation’s independence and the integrity of its territory.
International law, Treaty ratification, Foreign affairs representative

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 139
provisions.content:
He shall negotiate and sign all international treaties, conventions and agreements and submit them to the National Assembly for ratification.
Head of state powers, Foreign affairs representative

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 139-1
provisions.content:
He shall accredit ambassadors and special envoys to foreign powers, receive letters of accreditation from ambassadors of foreign powers and issued exequatur to consuls.
Treaty ratification, Foreign affairs representative, Power to declare/approve war, International law

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 140
provisions.content: He declares war, and negotiates and signs peace treaties with the approval of the National Assembly.

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 141
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
Designation of commander in chief, Head of state powers
The President of the Republic, appoints, after deliberation in the Council of Ministers, following approval of the Senate, the commander-in-chief of the Armed Forces of Haiti, the commander-in-chief of the National Police, the Ambassadors and Consuls General and the councils of administration of the autonomous organs.

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 142
provisions.content:
By a decree issued in the Council of Ministers, the President of the Republic appoints the directors general of the civil service, and delegates and vice delegates of Departments and Arrondissements.
He also appoints, with the approval of the Senate, Administrative Councils of Autonomous Agencies.
Designation of commander in chief

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 143
provisions.content:
The President of the Republic is the nominal head of the armed forces, but he never commands them in person.
Approval or veto of general legislation

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 144
provisions.content:
He has the seal of the Republic affixed to all laws and promulgates them within deadline stipulated by the Constitution. Before the expiration of that deadline, he may avail himself of his right of objection.
Head of state powers

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 145
provisions.content:
He sees to the enforcement of judicial decisions, pursuant to the law.
Power to pardon

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 146
provisions.content:
The President of the Republic has the right to perform and commute sentences in all res judicata cases, except for sentences handed down by the High Court of Justice as stipulated in this Constitution.
Head of state powers

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 147
provisions.content:
He may grant amnesty only for political matters as stipulated by law.
Powers of cabinet

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 148
provisions.content:
If the President finds it temporarily impossible to discharge his duties the Executive Authority shall be vested in the Council of Ministers under the Presidency of the Prime Minister, so long as the disability continues.
Head of state replacement

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 149
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
In case of vacancy of the Presidency of the Republic either by resignation, dismissal, death or in case of physical or mental permanent incapacity duly declared, the Council of Ministers, under the  presidency of the Prime Minister, exercises the Executive Power until the election of another President.
In this case, the ballot for the election of the new President of the Republic for the time that remains to complete the mandate takes place sixty (60) days at least and one hundred twenty (120) days at most after the beginning of the vacancy, in accordance with the Constitution and the electoral law.
In the case where the vacancy is produced from the fourth year of the presidential mandate, the National Assembly meets of office within the sixty (60) days which follow the vacancy to elect a new Provisional President of the Republic for the tine that remains to complete the mandate.

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 149-1
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
This President is reputed to have completed one presidential mandate.

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 149-2
provisions.content:
[Inserted by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
Legislative oversight of the executive
No procedure of interpellation of the Government may be initiated during the periods of temporary disability of the President of the Republic or of presidential vacancy. In the case where one such procedure has been initiated before the period, it is suspended.

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 150
provisions.content: The President of the republic shall have no powers other than those accorded to him by the Constitution.

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 151
provisions.content: At the opening of each annual session of the Legislature, the President of the Republic shall deliver a message to the Legislature on the State of the Nation. This message may not be debated.

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 152
provisions.content: The President of the Republic shall receive a monthly salary from the Public Treasury upon taking the oath of office.

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 153
provisions.content: The President of the Republic shall have his official residence in the National Palace, in the capital city, unless the seat of the Executive Power is moved.

provisions.chapter: SECTION B. Duties of the President of the Republic
provisions.title: Article 154
provisions.content: The President of the Republic presides over the Council of Ministers.

provisions.chapter: SECTION C. The Government
provisions.title: Article 155
provisions.content: The Government is composed of the Prime Minister, the Ministers and Secretaries of State. The Prime Minister is the head of the Government.

provisions.chapter: SECTION C. The Government
provisions.title: Article 156
provisions.content:
The Government conducts the policy of the Nation. It is responsible before Parliament under the terms stipulated by the Constitution.
Eligibility for head of government

provisions.chapter: SECTION C. The Government
provisions.title: Article 157

provisions.chapter: SECTION D. Powers of The Prime Minister
provisions.title: Article 158
provisions.content:
With the approval of the President, the Prime Minister shall choose the members of his Cabinet of Ministers and shall go before Parliament to obtain a vote of confidence on his declaration of general policy. The vote shall be taken in open ballot, and an absolute majority of both Chambers is required.
In the event of a vote of no-confidence by one of the two (2) Chambers, the procedure shall be repeated.
Head of government powers

provisions.chapter: SECTION D. Powers of The Prime Minister
provisions.title: Article 159
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
The Prime Minister executes the laws. In case of absence, the temporary disability of the President of the Republic or on his demand, the Prime minister presides over the Council of Ministers. He has the Regulatory Power, but he may never suspend, or interpret the laws, acts and decrees, or refrain from executing them.
His regulatory power is exercised by Order of the Prime Minister.
Head of government powers

provisions.chapter: SECTION D. Powers of The Prime Minister
provisions.title: Article 159-1
provisions.content:
In concert with the President of the Republic, he is responsible for national defense.
Head of government powers

provisions.chapter: SECTION D. Powers of The Prime Minister
provisions.title: Article 160
provisions.content:
The Prime Minister appoints and dismisses directly or by delegation Government officials, according to the provisions of the Constitution and the law on the general regulations for Government operations.
Legislative oversight of the executive

provisions.chapter: SECTION D. Powers of The Prime Minister
provisions.title: Article 161
provisions.content:
The Prime Minister and the Ministers may appear before the two (2) Chambers to support bills and the objections of the President of the Republic and to reply to interpellation.
Powers of cabinet

provisions.chapter: SECTION D. Powers of The Prime Minister
provisions.title: Article 162
provisions.content:
Acts of the Prime Minister are countersigned, if need by the Ministers responsible for enforcing them. The Prime Ministers may be assigned a Ministerial portfolio.
Head of government powers, Powers of cabinet

provisions.chapter: SECTION D. Powers of The Prime Minister
provisions.title: Article 163
provisions.content:
The Prime Minister and the Ministers are jointly responsible for the acts of the President of the Republic and of their ministers that they countersign. They are also responsible for enforcement of the laws in the areas of their competence.
Eligibility for cabinet, Head of government's role in the legislature

provisions.chapter: SECTION D. Powers of The Prime Minister
provisions.title: Article 164
provisions.content:
The duties of the Prime Minister and of a member of the Government are incompatible with membership in the Parliament. If such a case occurs, the member of Parliament must choose one duty or the other.
Head of government replacement

provisions.chapter: SECTION D. Powers of The Prime Minister
provisions.title: Article 165
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
In case of resignation of the Prime Minister, the Government remains in place to expedite current affairs until the assumption of the functions by his successor.
In case of permanent incapacity duly declared of the Prime Minister or of his resignation from the post for personal reasons, the President will choose an Interim Prime Minister from among the members of the ministerial cabinet while attending to the formation of a new Government within a time period not passing thirty (30) days.

provisions.chapter: SECTION E. The Ministers and Secretaries of State
provisions.title: Article 166
provisions.content:
The President of the Republic presides over the Council of Ministers. The number of Ministers may be no fewer than ten (10).
When he deems it necessary, the Prime Minister may appoints Secretaries of State to the Ministers.

provisions.chapter: SECTION E. The Ministers and Secretaries of State
provisions.title: Article 167
provisions.content: The number of Ministers is set by law.

provisions.chapter: SECTION E. The Ministers and Secretaries of State
provisions.title: Article 168
provisions.content:
Holding a ministerial post is incompatible with the exercise of all other public employment, except for higher education.
Powers of cabinet

provisions.chapter: SECTION E. The Ministers and Secretaries of State
provisions.title: Article 169
provisions.content: Ministers are responsible for the acts of the Prime Minister that they countersign. They are jointly responsible for enforcement of the laws.

provisions.chapter: SECTION E. The Ministers and Secretaries of State
provisions.title: Article 169-1
provisions.content: In no case may an oral or written order of the President of the Republic or of the Prime Minister release Ministers from the responsibilities of their office.

provisions.chapter: SECTION E. The Ministers and Secretaries of State
provisions.title: Article 170
provisions.content: The Prime Minister, the Ministers and the Secretaries of State receive monthly salaries established by the Budgetary law.

provisions.chapter: SECTION E. The Ministers and Secretaries of State
provisions.title: Article 171
provisions.content:
Ministers appoint certain categories of Government employees by delegation of the Prime Minister, according to the conditions set by the law on Government operations.
Cabinet removal, Legislative oversight of the executive

provisions.chapter: SECTION E. The Ministers and Secretaries of State
provisions.title: Article 172
provisions.content: When one of the two (2) Chambers during an interpellation calls into question the responsibility of a Minister by a vote of censure passed by an absolute majority of its members, the Executive shall recall the Minister.

provisions.chapter: SECTION E. The Ministers and Secretaries of State
provisions.title: Article 172-1

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 173
provisions.content: The Judicial Power shall be vested in the Supreme Court (Cour de Cassation), the Courts of Appeal, Courts of First Instance, Courts of Peace and special courts, whose number, composition, organization, operation and jurisdiction are set by law.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 173-1
provisions.content:
Civil rights cases are exclusively the competence of the courts.
Structure of the courts

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 173-2
provisions.content:
No court and no jurisdiction in disputed matters may be established except by law. No special court may be established under any name whatever.
Supreme court term length, Ordinary court term length

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 174
provisions.content:
Judges of the Supreme Court and the Courts of Appeal are appointed for ten (10) years. Judges of the Courts of First Instance are appointed for seven (7) years. Their term begins at the time they take their oath of office.
Ordinary court selection, Supreme court selection

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 175
provisions.content: Supreme Court justices are appointed by the President of the Republic form a list submitted by the Senate of three (3) persons per court seat. Judges of the Courts of Appeal and Courts of First Instance are appointed from a list submitted by the Departmental Assembly concerned; Justices of the Peace are appointed from a list draw up by the Communal Assemblies.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 176
provisions.content:
The law regulates the conditions required for serving as a judge at any level. A School of the Magistrature shall be established.
Supreme/ordinary court judge removal, Ordinary court term length, Supreme court term length

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 177
provisions.content: Judges of the Supreme Court, the Courts of Appeal and the Courts of First Instance are appointed for life. They may be removed from office only because of a legally determined abuse of authority or be suspended following and indictment leveled against them. They may not be reassigned, without their consent, even in the case of a promotion. Their service may be terminated during their term of office only in the event of a duly determined permanent physical or mental incapacity.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 178
provisions.content:
The Supreme Court does not try cases on their merits. Nevertheless, in all cases other than those submitted to a jury, when a case between the same parties is tried upon second appeal, even with an incidental plea of defense, the Supreme Court, accepting the appeal, shall not remand the case to a lower court but shall rule on the merits, sitting as a full court.
Right to appeal judicial decisions

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 178-1
provisions.content: However, in the case of appeals from temporary restraining orders or orders of examining magistrates, grants of appeal pronounced in connection with such orders or from final sentences of the Peace Courts or decisions of special courts, the Supreme Court, admitting the appeal, shall pronounce a decision without remanding the case.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 179
provisions.content:
The duties of a judge are incompatible with any other salaried duties, except for education.
Right to public trial

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 180
provisions.content: Court proceedings are public. However, they may take place in closed session in the interest of public order and good morals, at the decision of the Court.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 180-1
provisions.content:
Sentences may not be delivered in closed session in cases of political offenses or offenses involving the press.
Supreme court opinions

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 181
provisions.content:
All order or judgments shall state the grounds for the decision and shall be handed down in a public hearing.
Attorney general

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 181-1
provisions.content:
Orders or judgments are delivered and executed in the name of the Republic, They shall include writs of execution to officers of the Public Prosecutor’s Office and agents of the police and armed forces. Acts of notaries shall be put in the same form when their compulsory execution is involved.
Supreme court powers

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 182
provisions.content:
The Supreme court rules on conflicts of jurisdiction, in the manner regulated by law.
Establishment of military courts

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 182-1
provisions.content: The Supreme Court rules on both fact and law in all cases of decisions handed down by military courts.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 183
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 183-1
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 183-2
provisions.content: The Courts shall apply Government decrees and regulations only insofar as they are in conformity with the law.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 184
provisions.content:
The law determines the jurisdiction of the courts and tribunals, and regulates the manner of proceedings before them.
Attorney general

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 184-1
provisions.content:
The law also provides for disciplinary penalties to be taken against judges and officers of the Public Prosecutor’s Office, except for Supreme Court Justices, who are under the jurisdiction of the High Court of Justice for abuse of authority.
Establishment of judicial council

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. The Judicial Power
provisions.title: Article 184-2
provisions.content:
[Inserted by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
The administration and the control of the Judicial Power are entrusted to a Superior Council of the Judicial Power which exercises over the magistrates a right of surveillance and of discipline, and which has at its disposal a general power of information and of recommendation on the state of the magistrature.
The conditions of organization and of functioning of the Superior Council of the Judicial Power are established by the law.
Courts for judging public officials

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The High Court of Justice
provisions.title: Article 185
provisions.content: The Senate may constitute itself as a High Court of Justice. the proceedings of this Court are presided over by the President of the Senate, assisted by the President and Vice President of the Supreme Court as Vice President and Secretary, respectively, except where the Justices of the Supreme Court and officers of the public Prosecutor’s Office assigned to that court are involved in the accusation, in which case, the Senators, one of whom shall be designated by the accused, and the Senators so appointed shall not be entitled to vote.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The High Court of Justice
provisions.title: Article 186

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The High Court of Justice
provisions.title: Article 187
provisions.content:
Members of the High Court of Justice serve on an individual bases, and no opening proceedings, take the following oath;
“I swear before God and before the Nation to judge with the impartiality and the firmness appropriate to an honest and free man, according to my conscience and my deep-seated conviction”.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The High Court of Justice
provisions.title: Article 188
provisions.content:
The High Court of Justice shall designate, by secret ballot and an absolute majority of votes, from among its members a Committee of Enquiry.
Head of state removal, Cabinet removal, Head of government removal, Supreme/ordinary court judge removal

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The High Court of Justice
provisions.title: Article 188-1
provisions.content: The decision in the form of a decree shall be handed down on the report of the Committee of Enquiry by a two-thirds (2/3) majority of the members of the High Court of Justice.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The High Court of Justice
provisions.title: Article 189
provisions.content:
The High Court of Justice shall not sit unless a majority of two-thirds (2/3) of its members are present.
Head of government removal, Supreme/ordinary court judge removal, Head of state removal, Cabinet removal

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The High Court of Justice
provisions.title: Article 189-1
provisions.content:
The Court may not impose any other penalties than dismissal, disqualification or deprivation of the right or exercise any public office for no less than five (5) years and no more than fifteen (15) years.
Head of government immunity, Head of state immunity

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The High Court of Justice
provisions.title: Article 189-2
provisions.content: However, the convicted person may be brought before ordinary courts, in accordance with the law, if there is reason to impose other penalties or to rule on the institution of civil action.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The High Court of Justice
provisions.title: Article 190
provisions.content: Once a case is brought before the High Court of Justice, the Court must sit until it renders its verdict, regardless of the length of the sessions of the Legislature.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Permanent Electoral Council
provisions.title: Article 191
provisions.content: The Permanent Electoral Council is responsible for organizing and controlling with complete independence all electoral procedures throughout the territory of the Republic until the results of the election are announced.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Permanent Electoral Council
provisions.title: Article 191-1
provisions.content: The Council also drafts the Electoral Bill that it submits to the Executive Power for the necessary purposes.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Permanent Electoral Council
provisions.title: Article 191-2
provisions.content: The Council sees to it that the electoral lists are kept up-to-date.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Permanent Electoral Council
provisions.title: Article 192

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Permanent Electoral Council
provisions.title: Article 193

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Permanent Electoral Council
provisions.title: Article 194
provisions.content: Members of the Permanent Electoral Council are appointed for a nine (9) year nonrenewable period. They may not be removed from office.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Permanent Electoral Council
provisions.title: Article 194-1
provisions.content:
One-third of the members of the Permanent Electoral Council are replaced every three (3) years. The President is chosen from among its members.
Oaths to abide by constitution

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Permanent Electoral Council
provisions.title: Article 194-2
provisions.content:
Before taking office, the members of the Permanent Electoral Council take the following oath before the Supreme Court;
“I swear to respect the Constitution and the provisions of the Electoral Law and to discharge my duties with dignity, independence, impartiality and patriotism.”

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Permanent Electoral Council
provisions.title: Article 195
provisions.content: In the event of a serious offense committed in the discharge of their duties, the members of the Permanent Electoral Council are liable for prosecution before the High Court of Justice.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Permanent Electoral Council
provisions.title: Article 195-1
provisions.content: The seat of the Permanent electoral Council is in the capital. Its jurisdiction extends throughout the territory of the Republic.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Permanent Electoral Council
provisions.title: Article 196
provisions.content:
Members of the Permanent electoral Council may not hold any other public post, nor may they be a candidate of an elective post during their term.
In the event of dismissal, a member of the Council must wait three (3) years before he may run for an elective post.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Permanent Electoral Council
provisions.title: Article 197
provisions.content: The Permanent Electoral Council shall rule on all disputes arising either in elections or in the enforcement or the violation of the Electoral Law, subject to any legal prosecution undertaken against an offender or offenders before the courts of competent jurisdiction.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Permanent Electoral Council
provisions.title: Article 198
provisions.content: In the event of a vacancy caused by a depth, resignation or any other reason, the member shall be replaced following the procedure established in Article 192 for the remainder of his term, taking into account the power of government that had designated the member to be replaced.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Permanent Electoral Council
provisions.title: Article 199
provisions.content:
The law determines the rules for organization and operation of the Permanent Electoral Council
Establishment of administrative courts

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes
provisions.title: Article 200
provisions.content: The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes is an independent and autonomous financial and administrative court. It is responsible for administrative and jurisdictional control of Government receipts and expenditures, verification of the accounts of the Government enterprises and of the territorial divisions.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes
provisions.title: Article 200-1
provisions.content: The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes hears cases against the State and the territorial divisions, the Administration and Government officials, public services and citizens.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes
provisions.title: Article 200-2
provisions.content: Its decisions are not subject to appeal, except to the Supreme Court.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes
provisions.title: Article 200-3

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes
provisions.title: Article 200-4
provisions.content:
The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes participates in drawing up the budget and is consulted on all matters concerning legislation on public finances and on all draft financial or commercial contracts, agreements and conventions to which the State is a party. It has the right to conduct audits in all Government agencies.
Eligibility for administrative  judges

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes
provisions.title: Article 200-5

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes
provisions.title: Article 200-6
provisions.content:
Candidates for membership on the Court shall submit their applications directly to the Office of the Senate of the Republic. The Senate elects the ten (10) members of the Court, who select the Court’s President and Vice President form among them.
Administrative court term length

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes
provisions.title: Article 201
provisions.content:
Court members have a ten (10) year term and may not be removed
Oaths to abide by constitution

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes
provisions.title: Article 202
provisions.content:
Before taking office, the members of the Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative disputes shall take the following oath before a section of the Supreme Court:
“I swear to respect the Constitution and the laws of the Republic, to discharge my duties properly and loyally and to conduct myself at all times with dignity”.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes
provisions.title: Article 203
provisions.content: Members of the Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes are under the jurisdiction of the High Court of Justice for any serious offenses committed in the discharge of their duties.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes
provisions.title: Article 204
provisions.content: The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes shall submit each year to the Legislature within thirty (30) days following the opening of the first legislative session a complete report on the country’s financial situation and on the efficacy of Government expenditures.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Superior Court of Auditors and Administrative Disputes
provisions.title: Article 205
provisions.content: The organization of the above-mentioned court, its membership regulations and its mode of operation are established by law.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER III. The Conciliation Commission
provisions.title: Article 206
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER III. The Conciliation Commission
provisions.title: Article 206-1
provisions.content:
[Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
Ombudsman

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. Protection of Citizens
provisions.title: Article 207
provisions.content: An office known as the OFFICE OF CITIZEN PROTECTION is established to protect all individuals against any form of abuse by the government.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. Protection of Citizens
provisions.title: Article 207-1
provisions.content: The office is directed by a citizen bearing the title of PROTECTOR OF CITIZENS. He is chosen by consensus of the President of the Republic, the President of the Senate and the President of the Chamber of Deputies. His term is seven (7) years and may not be renewed.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. Protection of Citizens
provisions.title: Article 207-2
provisions.content: His intervention on behalf of any complainant is without charge, whatever the court having jurisdiction might be.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. Protection of Citizens
provisions.title: Article 207
provisions.content:
[Inserted by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
In the exercise of its functions, it will pay a special attention to the complaints presented by women, particularly in that relating to the discriminations and the aggressions of which they may be victims notably in their work.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER IV. Protection of Citizens
provisions.title: Article 207-3
provisions.content: A law sets the conditions and regulations for the operation of the Office of Citizen Protection.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 208
provisions.content: Higher education is free. it is provided by the University of the Haitian State (Univertite d’Etat d’Haiti), which is autonomous and by the superior public schools and the superior private schools accredited by the State.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 209
provisions.content: The State must finance the operation and development of the Haitian State University and the public superior schools. Their organization and their location must be planned from the perspective of regional development.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 210
provisions.content: The Establishment of research centers must be encouraged.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 211
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
Reference to science
A public organ is created responsible for the regulation and the control of quality of the superior education and of the scientific research throughout the territory. This organ exercises its control over all the public and non-public institutions working in these two domains. Each year, it publishes a report on the quality of the training and establishes a list of the performing institutions. The law determines the denomination, and establishes the mode of organization and the functioning of this organ.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 211-1
provisions.content: The universities and the private and public superior schools provide academic and practical instruction adapted to the trends and requirements of national development.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 212
provisions.content: An organic law regulates the establishment, location and operation of university and public and private superior schools in the country.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 213
provisions.content: A Haitian Academy shall be established to standardize the Creole language and enable it to develop scientifically and harmoniously.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 213-1
provisions.content: Other academies may be established.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 214
provisions.content: The title Academy Member is purely honorific.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 214-1
provisions.content:
The law shall determine the mode of organization and operation of academies.
Right to culture

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 215
provisions.content: Archaeological, historical, cultural, folklore and architectural treasures in the country, which bear witness to the grandeur of our past are part of the national heritage. Consequently, monuments, ruins, sites of our ancestors’ great feats of arms, famous centers of our African beliefs, and all vestiges of the past are placed under the protection of the State.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 216
provisions.content: The law determines special conditions for this protection in each sphere.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 217
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
The finances of the Republic include two components: the national finances and the local finances. Their respective management is assured by the organs and mechanisms specified to that effect.
The Executive Power is held to specify a mode of consultation of the territorial collectivities for any procedure of interest to the local finances.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 218
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
Subsidiary unit government, Municipal government
No tax in favor of the State may be established except by a law. No charge, no imposition, whether departmental, whether municipal, or whether of communal section, may be established without the consent of those territorial collectivities.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 219
provisions.content:
No preferential tax treatment may be established.
No tax exemption, increase, decrease or elimination may be established except by law.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 220
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
No pension, no gratification, no allocation, no subvention, at the charge of the Public Treasury, may be granted but by virtue of a law. The indexing of the pensions paid by the State will be established following the rhythm of the augmentation of the emoluments of the functionaries of the State.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 221
provisions.content: Subject to special provisions thereon, the holding of two or more salaried public offices at the same time is strictly forbidden, except posts in education.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 222
provisions.content: Procedures for preparation of the budget and its execution are determined by law.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 223
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
The execution of the Law of Finance is govern by the laws on the budget and public accounting and is assured by the services specified by the law.
The control of the execution of the Law of Finance is assured by the Parliament, the Superior Court of Accounts and any other institutions specified by the law.
Central bank

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 224
provisions.content:
National monetary policy is set by the Central Bank jointly with the Minister of Economics and Finance.
Central bank

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 225
provisions.content:
An autonomous public agency with legal personality and financial autonomy performs the functions of a Central Bank. Its regulations are determined by law.
Central bank

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 226
provisions.content: The Central Bank has exclusive authority to issue as legal tender throughout the territory of the Republic, paper money representing the monetary unit, and coins, according to the name, weight, description, amount and use set by law.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 227
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
The budget is voted by administrative entity following the classification established by the law.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 227-1
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 227-2
provisions.content: General accounts of receipts and expenditures of the Republic shall be kept by the Minister of Finance according to an accounting method established by law.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 227-3
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
The general accounts and the budgets prescribed by the preceding Article, accompanied by the report of the Superior Court of Accounts and of the Administrative Disputes must be submitted to the Legislative Chambers by the Minister in charge of finance within the time periods established by the law.
It is the same for the annual balance sheet and for the operations of the Central Bank, as well as for all other accounts of the State.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 227-4
provisions.content: The Government fiscal year begins on October 1 of each year and ends on September 30 of the following year.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 228
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
Each year, the Legislative Power orders:
the account of the receipts and of the expenses of the State for the past year or the preceding years;
the general budget of the State.
Budget bills

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 228-1
provisions.content: However, no proposal or amendment may be introduced into the Budget when it is being voted upon, without provision of the ways and means therefore.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 228-2
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 229
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 230
provisions.content:
Examination and payment of the General Administration Accounts and all accounts of public funds are effected according to the method established by law.
Budget bills

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 231
provisions.content: If for any reason whatever the Legislative Chambers do not act upon the budget for one or more Ministerial Departments before they adjourn, the budget or budgets of the Departments concerned shall remain in force until a new budget is voted on and adopted.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 231-1
provisions.content: In the event that, through fault of the Executive Power, the Budget of the Republic has not been voted upon, the President of the Republic shall immediately call a special session of the Legislative Chambers for the sole purpose of voting on the Government budget.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 232
provisions.content:
Autonomous agencies and enterprises and entities subsidized wholly or in part by the Public Treasury shall be governed by special budgets and salary and wage systems approved by the Executive Power.
Legislative committees

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 233
provisions.content:
For the purpose of maintaining constant and careful supervision over Government expenditures, a fifteen-member Parliamentary Committee with nine (9) Deputies and six (6) Senators shall be elected by secret ballot at the beginning of each regular session, to report on the management Ministers, in order to enable the two (2) Assemblies to give them discharge.
This Committee may engage the services of specialists to assist it with its monitoring functions.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 234
provisions.content: The Haitian Civil Service is the instrument by which the State carries out its missions and achieves its objectives. To ensure its viability, it must be managed honestly and efficiently.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 234-1
provisions.content:
[Inserted by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
The National Public Administration is constituted by the Administration of the State and by the Administration of the Territorial Collectivities.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 235
provisions.content: Government employees and officials shall be exclusively in the service of the State. It is their duty to abide faithfully by the norms and ethics determined by law for civil servants.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 236
provisions.content:
The law establishes the organization of the various Government structures and stipulates the conditions for their operation.
Civil service recruitment

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 236-1
provisions.content: The law shall regulate the civil service on the basis of aptitude, merit and conduct. It shall guarantee security of employment.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 236-2
provisions.content: The civil service is a career. No official may be hired except by competition or by meeting other conditions prescribed by the Constitution and by law, nor may he be dismissed except for causes specifically determined by law. Dismissals must in all cases be ruled upon by the Court of Administrative Disputes.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 237
provisions.content: Career service officials are not members of any particular Government agency but are members of the civil service, which makes them available to the various Government agencies.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 238
provisions.content:
Officials indicated by law have the obligation to declare the status of their net worth to the Clerk of the Civil Court within thirty (30) days following their entry into service. The Government Auditor must take every step he deems necessary to verify the accuracy of the declaration.
Freedom of association

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 239
provisions.content:
Government employees and officials may form associations to defend their rights under the conditions established by law.
Attorney general

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 240
provisions.content: Holders of public office or positions, particularly Ministers and Secretaries of State, officers of the Public Prosecutor’s Office, Delegates and Vice Delegates, ambassadors, private secretaries of the President of the Republic, members of the Cabinet of Ministers, the Director Generals of the Ministerial Department of autonomous agencies, and members of the Administrative Council are not eligible for the Government career service.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 241
provisions.content: The law punishes violations committed against the treasury and unjust gain. Officials who have knowledge of such actions have the duty to report them to the competent authorities.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 242
provisions.content: Unjust gain may be determined by all types of evidence, particularly presumption of a sharp disproportion between the official’s means acquired after his entry into service and the accumulated amount of salaries and emoluments to which the post he has occupied entitles him.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 243
provisions.content: Officials guilty of the above offenses are entitled to only the twenty-year statute of limitation. This limitation period begins to run with the termination of their duties or the causes that would have prevented any prosecution.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER V. The University - The Academy - Culture
provisions.title: Article 244
provisions.content: The State has the duty to avoid major salary disparities in the civil service.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. Economics and Agriculture
provisions.title: Article 245
provisions.content:
Economic freedom shall be guaranteed so long as it is not contrary to the public interest.
The State shall protect private enterprises and shall endeavor to see that it develops under the conditions necessary to increase the national wealth in such a way as to ensure the participation of the largest possible number of persons in the benefits of this wealth.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. Economics and Agriculture
provisions.title: Article 246
provisions.content: The State encourages in rural and urban areas the formation of cooperatives for production, processing of raw materials and the entrepreneurial spirit to promote the accumulation of national capital to ensure continuous development.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. Economics and Agriculture
provisions.title: Article 247
provisions.content: Agriculture, which is the main source of the Nation’s wealth, is a guarantee of the well-being of the people and the socio-economic progress of the Nation.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. Economics and Agriculture
provisions.title: Article 248
provisions.content: A special agency to be known as THE NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF AGRARIAN REFORM shall be established to organize the revision of real property structures and to implement an agrarian reform to benefit those who actually work the land. This Institute shall draw up an agrarian policy geared to optimizing productivity by constructing infrastructure aimed at the protection and management of the land.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. Economics and Agriculture
provisions.title: Article 248-1
provisions.content: The law determines the minimum and maximum area of basic farm units.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. Economics and Agriculture
provisions.title: Article 249
provisions.content:
The State has the obligation to establish the structures necessary to ensure maximum productivity of the land and domestic marketing of foodstuffs. Technical and financial management units shall be established to assist farmers at the level of each Communal section.
Right to competitive marketplace

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. Economics and Agriculture
provisions.title: Article 250
provisions.content: No monopoly may be established to benefit the State and the territorial divisions except in the exclusive interest of society as a whole. Such a monopoly may not be granted to any private individual.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. Economics and Agriculture
provisions.title: Article 251
provisions.content: The import of foodstuffs and their byproducts that are produced in sufficient quantity in the national territory is forbidden, except in the event of force majeure.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. Economics and Agriculture
provisions.title: Article 252
provisions.content:
The State may take charge of the operation of enterprises for the production of goods and services essential to the community in order to ensure continuity in the event the existence of these establishments should be threatened. Such enterprises shall be grouped in a comprehensive management system.
Protection of environment

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.title: Article 253
provisions.content: Since the environment is the natural framework of the life of the people, any practices that might disturb the ecological balance are strictly forbidden.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.title: Article 253-1
provisions.content:
[Inserted by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
As long as the forest coverage remains below 10% of the national territory, measures of exception must be taken with a view of working to the restoration of the ecological equilibrium.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.title: Article 254
provisions.content: The State shall organize the enhancement of natural sites to ensure their protection and make them accessible to all.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.title: Article 255
provisions.content: To protect forest reserves and expand the plant coverage, the State encourages the development of local sources of energy: solar, wind and others.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.title: Article 256
provisions.content: Within the framework of protecting the environment and public education, the State has the obligation to proceed to establish and maintain botanical and zoological gardens at certain points in its territory.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.title: Article 256-1
provisions.content:
[Inserted by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
The State may, if the necessity for it is demonstrated, declare a zone of ecological utility.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.title: Article 257
provisions.content: The law specifies the conditions for protecting flora and fauna, and punishes violations thereof.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.title: Article 258
provisions.content: No one may introduce into the country wastes or residues of any kind from foreign sources.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.title: Article 259
provisions.content:
The State protects the family, which is the foundation of society.
Rights of children, State support for the elderly, Right to found a family, State support for children

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.title: Article 260
provisions.content:
It must also protect all families regardless of whether they are constituted within the bonds of marriage. It must endeavor to aid and assist mothers, children and the aged.
Rights of children

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.title: Article 261
provisions.content: The law ensures protection for all children. Any child is entitled to love, affection, understanding and moral and physical care grow its father and mother.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.title: Article 262
provisions.content: A family Code must be drawn up to ensure protection and respect for the rights of the family and to define procedures of the search for affiliation. Courts and other Government agencies charged with the protection of these rights must be accessible free of charge at the level of the smallest territorial division.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 263
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
The Public Force is composed of two (2) distinct corps:
the Armed Forces of Haiti;
the National Police of Haiti.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 263-1
provisions.content:
No other armed corps may exist in the national territory.
Oaths to abide by constitution

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Environment
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 263-2
provisions.content: All members of the police and armed forces shall take an oath of allegiance and respect for the Constitution and the flag at the time of their enlistment.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 264
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
The Armed Forces of Haiti include the Forces of the Land, of the Sea, of the Air and the technical services.
The Armed Forces of Haiti are constituted to guaranty the defense and the integrity of the territory of the Republic.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 264-1
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
Designation of commander in chief
The Armed Forces of Haiti are effectively commanded by an Officer General having as a title Commander-in-Chief.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 264-2
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
Designation of commander in chief
The Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces of Haiti, in accordance with the Constitution, is chosen from among the officers general on active service.
Designation of commander in chief

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 264-3
provisions.content: His term is set at three (3) years and is renewable.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 265
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
Restrictions on the armed forces
The Armed Forces of Haiti are apolitical. Their members may not be part of a group or of a political party and they must observe the strictest neutrality.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 265-1
provisions.content: Members of the Armed Forces exercise their right to vote, under the Constitution.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 266

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 267
provisions.content: Military personnel on active duty may not be appointed to any Government post, except temporarily to perform a specialized service.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 267-1
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
All military personnel on active service, to stand as a candidate to an elective function, must obtain their retirement or their resignation two (2) years before the elections.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 267-2
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
Selection of active-duty commanders
The military career is a profession. It is hierarchical. The conditions of employment, the ranks, promotions, dismissals, retirements, are determined by the regulations of the Armed Forces of Haiti.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 267-3
provisions.content:
[Amended by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
Establishment of military courts
Military personnel are only justiciable by a military court for the misdemeanors and crimes committed in time of war or for the infractions arising in the military discipline.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 267-4
provisions.content: Military personnel retain for life the last rank obtained in the Haitian Armed Forces. They may be deprived of their rank only by a final judgment by a court of competent jurisdiction.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 267-5
provisions.content:
The State must award benefits to military personnel of all ranks, fully guaranteeing their physical security.
Duty to serve in the military

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 268
provisions.content:
Within the framework of compulsory civilian national services for both sexes, provided for by Article 52-3 of the Constitution, the Armed Forces participate in organizing and supervising that service.
Military service is compulsory for all Haitians who have attained eighteen (18) years of age.
The law sets the method of recruitment, and the length and regulations for the performance of these services.
Right to bear arms

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 268-1
provisions.content: Every citizen has the right to armed self-defense, within the bounds of this domicile, but has no right to bear arms without express well-founded authorization from the Chief of Police.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 268-2
provisions.content: Possession of a firearm must be reported to the police.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER I. The Armed Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 268-3
provisions.content: The Armed Forces have a monopoly on the manufacture, import, export, use and possession of weapon of war and their munitions, as well as war material.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 269
provisions.content:
The Police Force is an armed body.
It operates under the Ministry of Justice.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 269-1
provisions.content:
It is established to ensure law and order and protect the life and property of citizens.
Its organization and mode of operation are regulated by law.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 270
provisions.content: The Commander in Chief of the Police Forces is appointed, in accordance with the Constitution, for a three (3) year term, which is renewable.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 271
provisions.content: An Academy and a Police School have been established, whose organization and operations are set by law.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 272
provisions.content: Specialized sections, particularly the Penitentiary Administration, the Firemen’s Service, the Traffic Police, the Highway Police, Criminal Investigations, the Narcotics Service and the Anti-Smuggling Service, have been established by the law governing the organization, operation and location of the Police Forces.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 273
provisions.content: The police, as an auxiliary of the Justice System, investigate violations, offenses and crimes committed, in order to discover and arrest the perpetrators of them.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XI. The Armed Forces and the Police Force
provisions.title: Article 274
provisions.content: In the exercise of their duties, members of the “Public Forces” are subject to civil and penal liability in the manner and under the conditions stipulated by the Constitution and by law.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 275
provisions.content: National and legal holidays shall be celebrated by the Government and private and commercial enterprises.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 275-1

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 275-2
provisions.content:
Legal holidays shall be determined by law.
Treaty ratification, Legal status of treaties

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 276
provisions.content:
The National Assembly may not ratify any international treaty, convention or agreement containing clauses contrary to this Constitution.
Treaty ratification

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 276-1
provisions.content:
International treaties, conventions and agreements are ratified in the form of a decree.
Legal status of treaties, International law

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 276-2
provisions.content:
Once international treaties or agreements are approved and ratified in the manner stipulated by the Constitution, they become part of the legislation of the country and abrogate any laws in conflict with them.
Regional group(s)

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 277
provisions.content: The Haitian State may join an Economic Community of States insofar as the association agreement stimulates the social and economic development of the Haitian Republic and does not contain any clause contrary to this Constitution.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 278
provisions.content:
No place or part of the territory may be declared in a state of siege except in the event of civil war or invasion by a foreign force.
Emergency provisions

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 278-1
provisions.content:
The act of the President of the Republic declaring a state of siege must be countersigned by the Prime Minister and by all of the Ministers and contain an immediate convocation of the National Assembly to decide on the desirability of the measure.
Emergency provisions

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 278-2
provisions.content:
The National Assembly decides with the Executive Power as to what constitutional guarantees may be suspended in the parts of the territory placed under a state of siege.
Emergency provisions

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 278-3
provisions.content:
The state of siege is lifted if it is not renewed by a vote of the National Assembly every fifteen (15) days after its entry into force.
Emergency provisions

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 278-4
provisions.content: The National Assembly shall be in session for the entire duration of the state of siege.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 279
provisions.content: Thirty (30) days after his election, the President of the Republic must deposit with the Clerk of the Court of First Instance of his domicile a notarized inventory of all his movable and immovable goods, and he shall do the same at the end of his term.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 279-1
provisions.content: The Prime Minister, the Ministers and Secretaries of State are subject to the same obligation within thirty (30) days of their installation and of the termination of their duties.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 280
provisions.content:
No general expenditures or compensation whatever shall be granted to members of the major organs of the State for any special duties that may be assigned to them.
Campaign financing

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 281
provisions.content:
In national elections, the State assumes responsibility, in proportion to the number of votes cast, for a portion of the expenses incurred in the election campaigns.
Campaign financing

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 281-1
provisions.content:
Only parties that obtain nationally ten percent (10%) of the votes cast, with a minimum of five percent (5%) of the votes cast in one Department, are eligible to receive these Government funds.
Constitution amendment procedure

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XIII. Amendments to the Constitution
provisions.title: Article 282
provisions.content: On the recommendation, with reason given to support it, of one of the two (2) Chambers or of the Executive Power, the Legislature may declare that the Constitution should be amended.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XIII. Amendments to the Constitution
provisions.title: Article 282-1
provisions.content: This declaration must be supported by two-thirds (2/3) of each of the two (2) Chambers. It may made only in the course of the last Regular Session of the Legislative period and shall be published immediately throughout the territory.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XIII. Amendments to the Constitution
provisions.title: Article 283
provisions.content: At the first session of the following legislature period, the Chambers shall meet in a National Assembly and decide on the proposed amendment.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XIII. Amendments to the Constitution
provisions.title: Article 284
provisions.content: The National Assembly may not sit or deliberate on the amendment unless at least two-thirds (2/3) of the members of each of the two (2) Chambers are present.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XIII. Amendments to the Constitution
provisions.title: Article 284-1
provisions.content: No decision of the National Assembly may be taken without a majority of two- thirds (2/3) of the votes cast.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XIII. Amendments to the Constitution
provisions.title: Article 284-2
provisions.content: The amendment passed may enter into effect only after installation of the next elected President. In no case may the President under the Government that approved the amendment benefit from any advantages deriving there from.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XIII. Amendments to the Constitution
provisions.title: Article 284-3
provisions.content:
General elections to amend the Constitution by referendum are strictly forbidden.
Unamendable provisions

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.section: TITLE XIII. Amendments to the Constitution
provisions.title: Article 284-4
provisions.content:
No amendment to the Constitution may effect the democratic and republican nature of the State.
Transitional provisions

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 285
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 285-1
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 286
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 287
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 288
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 289

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 289-1
provisions.content: Within two weeks following ratification of this Constitution, the bodies or organizations concerned shall inform the Executive of the name of their representative.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 289-2
provisions.content: If any of the above bodies or organizations does not appoint a member, the Executive shall fill the vacancy or vacancies.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 289-3
provisions.content: The mission of the Provisional Electoral Council shall end when the President- elect takes office.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 290
provisions.content: The members of the first Permanent Electoral Council shall divide among them by lot the terms of nine (9), six (6), and three (3) years, stipulated for replacement of the Council by thirds (1/3).

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 291
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 292
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 293
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 293-1
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 294
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 295
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 295-1
provisions.content:
[Inserted by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]
At the time of the first composition of the Constitutional Council, the first three members appointed from the list of the Executive Power, of the National Assembly, and of the Superior Council of the Judicial Power will be for nine (9) years, the second three members for six (6) years and the other three for three (3) years.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 296
provisions.content: All Codes of Law or Handbooks of Justice, all laws, all decree laws and all decrees and orders (Arretes) currently in force shall be maintained in all matters not contrary to this Constitution.

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 297
provisions.content: [Abrogated by the Constitutional Law of 9 May 2011 / 19 June 2012]

provisions.chapter: CHAPTER II. The Police Forces
provisions.title: Article 298
provisions.content:
This Constitution shall be published within two weeks of its ratification by referendum. It shall enter into force as soon as it is published in LE MONITEUR, the Official Gazette of the Republic.
Given at the Legislative Palace, in Port-au-Prince, the seat of the Constituent National Assembly, on March 10, 1987, in the One Hundred Eighty-Fourth Year of Independence.
